About The Apiary Guide

Learn about The Apiary Guide's mission to help beekeepers thrive. Honest reviews, expert guidance, and transparent affiliate partnerships since day one.

The Apiary Guide exists to help beekeepers at every level make confident decisions about their hives. We're beekeepers first, writers second—and we only recommend products and practices we'd use in our own apiaries. Here's our story and how we operate.

Our Mission: Real Beekeeping Guidance, No Fluff

If you've spent time searching for beekeeping advice online, you've probably noticed a pattern: outdated information mixed with overly technical jargon, all wrapped up in aggressive sales pitches. That's the gap we're here to fill.

The Apiary Guide exists to provide practical, tested advice that respects both bees and beekeepers. We prioritize bee health above all else, advocate for sustainable practices that work with nature rather than against it, and help people avoid the costly beginner mistakes we've made ourselves.

Our mission is simple: give you the information you need to keep healthy hives, without the noise.

Who We Are

The Apiary Guide is built by experienced beekeepers with combined decades of hands-on experience managing hives across different climates, seasons, and contexts. We're not academics publishing from a lab, and we're not commercial operations with a bottom line that conflicts with yours.

We're hobbyists and sideliners who understand what it's really like to manage bees in a backyard, a small farm, or a suburban setting. We know the frustration of a failed package installation, the satisfaction of a strong spring buildup, and the real cost of equipment mistakes.

We're also committed to ongoing learning. Beekeeping science evolves, new products emerge, and our own practices improve every season. That's reflected in how we approach every guide and review on this site.

How We Test and Review Products

We don't review products based on marketing claims or manufacturer specs. Here's how we actually do it:

  • We buy and field-test equipment ourselves. If we recommend a hive tool, smoker, or feeder, we've used it in our own apiaries.
  • We consult with other beekeepers. We reach out to experienced keepers in our networks to understand how products perform over time and across different setups.
  • We evaluate durability, ease of use, and value. A product might be excellent, but if it costs three times more than a comparable alternative, we'll tell you that.
  • We don't accept payment for positive reviews. No manufacturer can pay us to recommend their product. Period.
  • We update reviews as things change. If a product improves or a better option emerges, we revise our recommendations. If something we recommended starts failing, you'll hear about it.

Transparency isn't just a buzzword for us—it's how we operate.

Affiliate Disclosure: How We Keep the Lights On

The Apiary Guide earns commissions when you purchase through our affiliate links. We primarily work with Amazon Associates and select beekeeping suppliers who share our values.

Here's what's important to understand: this commission structure does not influence our recommendations. We only link to products we genuinely recommend. If we wouldn't use it in our own hives, it won't get an affiliate link on this site—even if the commission would be substantial.

Why do we use affiliate links at all? Because they allow us to keep our content free and ad-light. No pop-up ads, no sponsored content masquerading as guides, no paywalls. Affiliate income is how we maintain the site, invest in research and testing, and keep improving our resources.

When you buy through our links, you pay the same price you would anywhere else. The commission comes from the retailer, not from your pocket. And we appreciate it—it means you're supporting independent beekeeping education.

What We Stand For

The Apiary Guide is built on a few core principles:

  • Bee-first practices. The health and welfare of the bees come before convenience, profit, or tradition. We oppose exploitative beekeeping methods and promote treatments that minimize harm.
  • Beginner-friendly education. Beekeeping shouldn't require a PhD to understand. We explain concepts clearly and avoid unnecessary jargon.
  • Environmental stewardship. Healthy bees live in healthy ecosystems. We advocate for practices that support pollinator populations and local environments.
  • Honest commerce. We believe in transparent affiliate partnerships and genuine product recommendations. We respect your intelligence and your budget.

We're here to help you become a better beekeeper—one who respects both the craft and the insects.

Get in Touch

We'd love to hear from you. Have a question about beekeeping? Found an error in one of our guides? Want to suggest a product we should review? Reach out.

You can contact us at hello@theapiaryguide.com. We aim to respond within 48 hours. We read every message, and your feedback helps us improve the site.

We're also committed to building a community of thoughtful beekeepers. If you have insights to share or corrections to suggest, we want to hear them. This site is better because of reader engagement.

Frequently Asked Questions

Does The Apiary Guide sell beekeeping products directly?

No. We don't sell anything directly. We create guides and reviews, and we link to products we recommend through affiliate partnerships. This keeps us independent and focused on what we do best: helping you make informed decisions.

How does The Apiary Guide make money?

We earn affiliate commissions when readers purchase through our links, primarily through Amazon Associates and select beekeeping suppliers. This income allows us to keep our content free, ad-light, and focused on quality. You pay the same price whether you buy through us or directly—the commission comes from the retailer.

Are your product reviews biased because of affiliate links?

No. We only recommend products we'd actually use in our own apiaries, regardless of commission potential. If a product isn't worth recommending, it won't get an affiliate link—even if the commission would be large. We've turned down lucrative partnerships because they didn't align with our values.

Can I trust the beekeeping advice on this site?

Yes, with the understanding that beekeeping is regional and contextual. Our advice is based on hands-on experience, consultation with experienced beekeepers, and current best practices. We always encourage you to adapt recommendations to your climate, local regulations, and specific situation. When in doubt, consult a local beekeeper or extension service.

Who writes the content for The Apiary Guide?

Our team consists of experienced beekeepers with combined decades of hands-on experience. We're hobbyists and sideliners, not academics or commercial operations. We write about what we know from direct experience, and we're always learning.
